atoms.semantic_claim: CanonicalInMemoryState stores a blocking notifier alongside the async broadcast sender.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Register a blocking canonical-state handler and commit a chain update; the handler should be retained and callable before async broadcast.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace storage for blocking canonical-state handlers in the v2.2 chain-state path.
atoms.notes: No blocking notifier field or equivalent was found in the v2.2 port.

atoms.semantic_claim: CanonicalInMemoryState exposes an API to register synchronous canonical-state handlers.
atoms.required_change: Decide whether the v2.2 chain-state API should expose blocking canonical-state subscription.
atoms.notes: No subscribe_blocking_canon_state or equivalent API was found in the v2.2 port.

atoms.semantic_claim: Blocking canonical-state handlers run synchronously before async canonical-state notifications are broadcast.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Install a blocking handler that records execution, trigger notify_canon_state, and assert the handler observes the committed chain before any async receiver is notified.
atoms.required_change: Restore or explicitly reject the old blocking-before-async canonical notification ordering requirement.
atoms.notes: The current v2.2 notify_canon_state directly broadcasts async notifications without a blocking pre-hook.

summary: Blocking canonical-state notification support from old chain-state is missing in the v2.2 port.
notes: This is the first real missing behavior in this batch and should be tied to downstream users of canonical-state update hooks.
